title: The Queen of Air and Darkness
summary: The heroes chase Darga the Hutt to Cloud City on Bespin, infiltrate a Sabacc tournament to intercept an Imperial payment, and face a desperate hangar bay showdown that ends with an orbital battle and a moral choice that defines the party.

gearNotes.reason: Varth coaches from the ship throughout — tournament strategy, Arandis identification, code decryption. Casino is crawling with Imperial undercover agents. Open comms = dead.
Commandeering the Glorious Chariot and Z-95s under fire with 30 seconds before a Stormtrooper platoon arrives. No time to improvise if something doesn't start cleanly.
Casino shootout into hangar battle into orbital combat — sustained multi-scene fighting with zero rest.
Catwalks over Bespin's atmosphere at Refinery Platform 13 and the open hangar bay. Safety line if someone gets knocked off — the drop is infinite.
Refinery recon ('they haven't seen you yet') and spotting Arandis's bodyguard positioning at the hotel.
Three weaponized dealer droids with hidden blaster cannons plus a heavy security droid at the hangar. Four droid encounters in one adventure.
Casino three-way firefight, city chase, and hangar assault — sightline control across three consecutive combat scenes.
Maintaining a convincing high-roller identity over a multi-day tournament. Also useful if Kessra from Adv2 shows up as hired muscle and might recognize a face.
Bugging Arandis's hotel conversations or his comms with the Stormtrooper detail.
Intercepting communications between Arandis and his handlers. Also useful during the chase to monitor Wing Guard frequencies.
Documenting Arandis's deliberate losing patterns as evidence of the Imperial payment scheme.
Sealed blast doors during the city chase if Varth's hack fails. Also cutting into the yacht if Darga seals the boarding ramp.
Hangar bay area denial against clustered pilots scrambling to Z-95 cockpits.
Casino surveillance avoidance when infiltrating as droid maintenance crew. Also useful during the Gauntlet chase.
Patching up between the casino firefight and the hangar assault. No time to rest.
Disabling the yacht's engines from outside if the heroes choose not to board.

scenes.title: The Brick Wall
scenes.readAloud:
Cloud City rises from the roiling orange clouds of Bespin like a crown of durasteel. In this era, it is not a polished resort — it is a rough-and-tumble mining boomtown, a Wild West in the clouds. Prospectors, gamblers, and fugitives pack the Cloudscape Platforms.

You hit the streets to find Darga — but the underworld is sealed tight. Every contact clams up. Then your datapads beep with an unsigned message:

"Stop digging. The drop is long, and the gas pressure is high. Go home."
scenes.gmNotes: Cloud City in 16 BBY is a mining boomtown, not Lando's resort. The heroes face a wall of silence — Darga has bribed the Wing Guard and port officials. The threatening message traces to the Parliament of Guilds (Ugnaught labor leadership), not Darga. Varth acts as 'The Man in the Chair,' analyzing data from the ship. The investigation leads to the Industrial District.

npcs.name: Local Contacts
npcs.notes: Tight-lipped. Darga has bribed everyone.

passive.name: Tight-Lipped
passive.description: Bribed or scared. Social checks to extract info are stepped up by 1 Power.

actions.name: Nervous Tip
actions.description: If trust is earned, provide one piece of intel.

maneuver.name: Disappear
maneuver.description: Walk away. Must be physically blocked or Charmed to continue conversation.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant social targets. Represent the wall of silence Darga has built.

scenes.hazards: Imperial spies. Darga's informant network. Wing Guard patrols.

decisions.consequence: Leads to the Ugnaught Parliament of Guilds in the Industrial District.
Risk attracting Darga's attention and Imperial surveillance.

skillChecks.context: Tracing the threatening message to its source.
skillChecks.success: Digital forensics trace the origin to the Ugnaught Parliament of Guilds — an industrial administrator terminal.
skillChecks.failure: Message was routed through multiple proxies. Dead end on the digital trail.

skillChecks.context: Getting locals to talk about Darga. Everyone is bribed or scared.
skillChecks.success: A bartender whispers: "Try the Ugnaughts in the Works. They hate him more than they fear him."
skillChecks.failure: Stone wall. Nobody talks. Darga's money is louder than your charm.

skillChecks.context: Moving through Cloud City without attracting Darga's informants.
skillChecks.success: Clean movement. No one flags your presence.
skillChecks.failure: A street rat clocks you and runs toward the cantina district. Darga may know you're here.

skillChecks.context: Creating a cover story for why you're on Cloud City.
skillChecks.success: Convincing cover as prospectors or traders.
skillChecks.failure: Cover is thin. First serious scrutiny will blow it.

skillChecks.context: Reading the threatening message — is it a real threat or a warning?
skillChecks.success: The tone is scared, not hostile. Whoever sent this is trying to protect themselves, not threaten you.
skillChecks.failure: Reads as a genuine threat. Approach with caution.

environmentMechanics.name: Darga's Informant Network
environmentMechanics.effect: Street-level contacts are bribed. Aggressive questioning reaches Darga within hours.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Work through intermediaries. Don't mention Darga by name.

environmentMechanics.name: Wing Guard Patrols
environmentMechanics.effect: Some Wing Guards are on Darga's payroll. Will report unusual activity.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Blend in. Don't carry visible weapons in tourist areas.

rewards.narrative: Trail leads to the Ugnaught Parliament of Guilds in the Industrial District.

pacing.openingBeat: Cloud City. A mining boomtown, not a resort. Every contact clams up.
pacing.risingAction: The threatening message arrives. Investigation begins.
pacing.climax: The trail converges on the Ugnaught Works.

scenes.title: The Industrial Connection
scenes.readAloud:
You descend into The Works — the noisy, steam-filled industrial bowels of Cloud City. The Ugnaughts who sent the threat aren't enemies; they're terrified administrators named Fyren and Krygg.

They admit they were trying to scare you away to prevent a gang war. They hate Darga. The Hutt is forcing Ugnaught riggers to siphon military-grade gas directly to Imperial ships, bypassing Guild quotas.

"Help us stop his thugs from stealing a shipment right now, and we'll tell you exactly how the handover at the tournament works."
scenes.gmNotes: The Ugnaughts are potential allies. They offer a trade: stop Darga's thugs from stealing a gas shipment at Refinery Platform 13, and they'll reveal the tournament handover structure. This establishes the Ugnaught labor exploitation angle and gives the heroes a clear objective.

npcs.name: Fyren
npcs.notes: Terrified but cooperative. Wants Darga gone.

passive.name: Guild Administrator
passive.description: Knows Cloud City's industrial infrastructure intimately. Can provide maps, schedules, and security gaps.

actions.name: Briefing
actions.description: Provide detailed tactical intel about refinery operations.

actions.name: Appeal
actions.description: Emotional plea for help. Makes moral cost of refusal clear.

maneuver.name: Pull Records
maneuver.description: Access Guild databases for shipping manifests and schedules.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant ally. His value is information and infrastructure access.

npcs.name: Krygg
npcs.notes: More cautious than Fyren. Needs convincing.

passive.name: Cautious Pragmatist
passive.description: Krygg won't commit until he sees action. Once convinced, he's reliable.

actions.name: Refuse
actions.description: Push back against requests until trust is earned.

maneuver.name: Consult
maneuver.description: Confer with other Ugnaughts about a request.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. More cautious than Fyren. Needs to see results before committing.

scenes.hazards: Industrial District — steam, noise, narrow catwalks. Imperial surveillance possible.

decisions.consequence: Leads to a tactical combat encounter at the refinery. Intel reward is worth it.
Ugnaughts refuse. They need proof the heroes are willing to act.

skillChecks.context: Convincing the Ugnaughts you're allies, not Darga's agents.
skillChecks.success: Fyren and Krygg open up. They describe the gas siphoning operation.
skillChecks.failure: They're not convinced. Demand proof of your intentions.

skillChecks.context: Reading Fyren and Krygg — are they trustworthy?
skillChecks.success: Genuine terror and anger. They've been exploited. This is real.
skillChecks.failure: Hard to read. Ugnaught body language is unfamiliar.

skillChecks.context: Reviewing the Ugnaughts' evidence of Darga's gas siphoning.
skillChecks.success: Shipping manifests confirm military-grade Tibanna going to Imperial warships. The scale is staggering.
skillChecks.failure: Evidence is circumstantial. Need more proof.

skillChecks.context: Building rapport with the Ugnaughts for a deeper alliance.
skillChecks.success: They commit fully. Will provide ongoing support — safe houses, equipment, information.
skillChecks.failure: They'll trade info for the refinery job but nothing more.

environmentMechanics.name: The Works
environmentMechanics.effect: Noisy industrial environment. Eavesdropping is nearly impossible — good for private conversations.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use the noise as cover for planning.

items.name: Ugnaught Alliance
items.note: Safe houses and information network in Cloud City's Industrial District.

rewards.narrative: Deal struck with the Ugnaughts. Refinery Platform 13 objective established.

pacing.openingBeat: Descend into the Works. Steam, noise, and Ugnaught territory.
pacing.risingAction: Fyren and Krygg reveal the gas siphoning operation.
pacing.climax: The deal: stop the theft at the refinery, get the tournament intel.
pacing.resolution: Alliance formed. Heroes prep for the refinery.

scenes.title: The Refinery Rumble
scenes.readAloud:
Refinery Platform 13 hangs deep in the gas giant's atmosphere. The wind howls across exposed catwalks, and steam vents obscure your vision. Below, there is nothing — just the infinite amber depths of Bespin.

On the platform, Trandoshan Mercenaries and corrupt Ugnaught riggers are loading gas canisters onto a shuttle. They haven't seen you yet.
scenes.gmNotes: Tactical combat on catwalks over a lethal drop. Stray shots risk rupturing gas tanks (explosions) or steam pipes (scald damage). The objective is to defeat the Trandoshans and secure the platform. A datapad on the Trandoshan leader confirms the gas is destined for the Imperial Navy and mentions a ship called the 'Shackles of Nizon.'

npcs.name: Trandoshan Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Darga's muscle. Loading stolen gas.

passive.name: Unrelenting
passive.description: Ignore first [Weakened] per scene.

maneuver.name: Scent Track
maneuver.description: Locate hidden enemies. Ignore concealment from steam.

gambit.name: Knockdown
gambit.description: Target gains [Prone]. Lethal near platform edges.

exploit.name: Regeneration
exploit.description: Heal 1 Vitality at end of turn.

threatBuild.notes: Same Trandoshan build as Adv2. On catwalks, their Knockdown gambit is potentially lethal — [Prone] near an edge.

npcs.name: Corrupt Ugnaught Riggers
npcs.notes: Non-combatants who flee if fighting starts.

passive.name: Coerced Workers
passive.description: These Ugnaughts are forced labor, not fighters. They flee immediately when combat starts.

maneuver.name: Surrender
maneuver.description: Drop tools and put hands up.

threatBuild.notes: Non-combatant. Will surrender or flee. Questioning them provides limited intel about Darga's operation.

scenes.hazards: Catwalks over a lethal drop. Gas tank explosions. Steam pipe scald damage. High winds.

decisions.consequence: Element of surprise. Risk of gas tank explosions in crossfire.

decisions.choice: Try to disable the shuttle first
decisions.consequence: Prevents escape but alerts the Trandoshans. Harder initial engagement.

skillChecks.context: Approaching the refinery platform undetected.
skillChecks.success: Perfect approach. Element of surprise secured.
skillChecks.failure: Spotted at the platform edge. Trandoshans scramble to defensive positions.

skillChecks.context: Ambush fire on the Trandoshan loading crew.
skillChecks.success: First volley drops one Trandoshan. Others scatter for cover.
skillChecks.failure: Shots go wide in the wind. Trandoshans return fire immediately.

skillChecks.context: Fighting on narrow catwalks in high winds.
skillChecks.success: Sure-footed. Move freely across the platform.
skillChecks.failure: Wind catches you. [Slowed] for 1 round or risk falling.

skillChecks.context: Disabling the shuttle to prevent the Trandoshans from escaping with the gas.
skillChecks.success: Shuttle engines shut down. No escape.
skillChecks.failure: Shuttle is still operational. Must be stopped physically.

skillChecks.context: Searching the Trandoshan leader's datapad after the fight.
skillChecks.success: Gas is destined for the Imperial Navy. Mentions "Shackles of Nizon" — a slave transport ship.
skillChecks.failure: Datapad is damaged. Partial data only.

encounters.name: Refinery Platform Ambush
encounters.description: 5 Trandoshan Mercenaries loading stolen Tibanna gas. Fight on catwalks over Bespin's atmosphere.
encounters.tactics: Trandoshans use gas canisters as cover. Shooting canisters causes explosions. Catwalks are narrow — no flanking.

environmentMechanics.name: Lethal Drop
environmentMechanics.effect: No ground beneath the catwalks — only Bespin's atmosphere. Any character knocked off a catwalk falls to their death unless caught.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Syntherope safety lines. Stay away from edges.

environmentMechanics.name: High Winds
environmentMechanics.effect: Athletics check at start of each round or [Slowed]. Ranged attacks at long range suffer -1 Power.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Close range combat. Melee is unaffected.

environmentMechanics.name: Tibanna Gas Tanks
environmentMechanics.effect: Explosion: [Hazard 3] in the zone. Destroys that section of catwalk.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't shoot near the tanks. Or do, tactically.

environmentMechanics.name: Steam Vents
environmentMechanics.effect: Random venting. Characters in zone gain [Concealment] but also [Hazard 1].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use for cover. Accept the minor burn.

items.name: Trandoshan Datapad
items.note: References "Shackles of Nizon" and Imperial Navy gas deliveries.

rewards.narrative: Refinery secured. Ugnaught deal honored. Tournament intel incoming.

pacing.openingBeat: The platform hangs in Bespin's atmosphere. Trandoshans loading gas below.
pacing.risingAction: The ambush. Fighting on catwalks in wind and steam.
pacing.climax: The shuttle — stop it from escaping with the gas.

scenes.title: The Imperial Mark
scenes.readAloud:
The Ugnaughts pay their debt. The handover isn't happening in secret — Darga insisted it occur in plain sight during the Cloud City Sabacc Tournament.

At the Yerith Bespin Hotel, you spot the Imperial Bagman: Lieutenant Armen Arandis. Dressed in civilian luxury but moving with military stiffness, flanked by 'bodyguards' who are clearly undercover Stormtroopers.

Varth's voice crackles in your ear: "That's the Bagman. He isn't here to play cards — he's here to transfer credits to Darga's proxy by losing intentionally. We need to be at that table."
scenes.gmNotes: Arandis is an Imperial math genius posing as a gambler. His job is to lose the pot intentionally to Darga's proxy. The heroes need to infiltrate the tournament to intercept the credits. The buy-in is massive (10,000 credits) — Varth provides the stake. Heroes must decide roles: The Player (gambler), The Security (hired guards), The Cheat (droid maintenance infiltrator).

npcs.name: Lt. Armen Arandis
npcs.notes: Mathematical genius. Cold, emotionless. Here to lose intentionally.

passive.name: Mathematical Precision
passive.description: Arandis calculates everything. +2 Power on Wits-based challenges. His Sabacc play is a transfer algorithm, not gambling.

actions.name: Cold Read
actions.description: Study a target. Learn one piece of intel about their cover or intentions.

maneuver.name: Analyze
maneuver.description: Study the situation. Next action gains +1 Power.

gambit.name: Probability Lock
gambit.description: In Sabacc: force a specific card outcome. In combat: predict enemy movement for guaranteed hit.

exploit.name: Dead Man's Switch
exploit.description: If killed, a pre-recorded message alerts the Assiduous.

threatBuild.notes: Operative. Disciplined: +1 Resist. Arandis is a genius pretending to be a gambler. His death triggers an alert to Draco.

npcs.name: Undercover Stormtroopers
npcs.notes: Arandis's bodyguards. Professional but trying to blend in.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

actions.name: Close Protection
actions.description: Grapple or restrain a threat to Arandis.

maneuver.name: Shield Arandis
maneuver.description: Interpose between Arandis and attacker.

gambit.name: Coordinated Fire
gambit.description: All Troopers on same target gain +1 Power.

exploit.name: Call Extraction
exploit.description: Summon Imperial shuttle. 10-minute ETA.

threatBuild.notes: Standard Stormtroopers in civilian disguise. No armor = reduced Defense. Same training, less protection.

scenes.hazards: Heavy hotel security. Imperial surveillance. High-profile public venue.

decisions.consequence: Direct access to the table. Requires gambling skill.
Free movement but no access to the game itself.
Can rig dealer droids. High-tech approach.

skillChecks.context: Identifying Arandis's bodyguards as undercover Stormtroopers.
skillChecks.success: Military bearing, identical boots, comm earpieces. Stormtroopers in civilian clothes.
skillChecks.failure: They look like professional bodyguards. Nothing unusual.

skillChecks.context: Creating a tournament entry identity — the Player persona.
skillChecks.success: Convincing high-roller identity with a fabricated fortune history.
skillChecks.failure: Identity is thin. Casino staff may flag it.

skillChecks.context: Casing the Yerith Bespin Hotel without being noticed.
skillChecks.success: Full layout mapped. Security camera blind spots identified.
skillChecks.failure: Hotel security notices your loitering. You're remembered.

skillChecks.context: Infiltrating the casino's droid maintenance network.
skillChecks.success: Access to dealer droid firmware. Can monitor or modify card dealing.
skillChecks.failure: Firewalled. Need physical access to a maintenance terminal.

skillChecks.context: Studying Arandis's playing pattern over surveillance.
skillChecks.success: He's not gambling — he's executing a mathematical transfer. Every loss is calculated.
skillChecks.failure: His play seems erratic. Hard to find a pattern.

environmentMechanics.name: Hotel Security
environmentMechanics.effect: Professional security. Cameras, badge checks, restricted floors.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Legitimate entry as tournament player or staff.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Surveillance
environmentMechanics.effect: Undercover Stormtroopers monitor all approaches. Counter-surveillance active.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't approach Arandis directly. Observe from distance.

rewards.narrative: Tournament roles assigned. The heist is planned.

pacing.openingBeat: The Ugnaughts deliver: Darga's payment happens at the Sabacc tournament.
pacing.risingAction: Arandis is identified. His bodyguards are Stormtroopers. Stakes escalate.
pacing.climax: The heroes choose their tournament roles.
pacing.resolution: Plan is set. Buy-in secured. The game begins.

scenes.title: The Buy-In
scenes.subtitle: Preparation
scenes.readAloud:
As you finalize your entry strategy, Varth's voice cuts in — tight with genuine fear.

"We have a problem. A massive energy spike just hit the system edge. An Imperial Star Destroyer just dropped out of hyperspace. It's the Assiduous — Inquisitor Draco's flagship."

A beat of silence.

"That ship is the clean-up crew. If Arandis loses the money wrong, or if Darga double-crosses them, Draco won't bother with arrests. He'll glass the city to bury the evidence. Win that pot and get the codes before Draco gets impatient."
scenes.gmNotes: The Assiduous arrives — escalating the stakes dramatically. Draco is the 'clean-up crew' for Project Leviathan. If the deal goes wrong, he'll destroy Cloud City to bury the evidence. This puts a hard clock on the entire tournament arc. The heroes must win the pot and get the gas codes before Draco loses patience.

npcs.name: Inquisitor Valin Draco
npcs.notes: Aboard the Assiduous in orbit. Prepared to 'glass the city' if needed.

passive.name: Dark Presence
passive.description: All enemies in the scene suffer -1 Power from fear unless they pass a Resolve check (T2/P0) at scene start.

actions.name: Lightsaber Assault
actions.description: Crimson lightsaber attack. +2 Power. Ignores non-cortosis armor.

actions.name: Force Choke
actions.description: Ranged Force attack. Target must Endure or gain [Stunned] and take damage.

maneuver.name: Force Leap
maneuver.description: Move 2 zones instantly. Ignore terrain and obstacles.

gambit.name: Force Lightning
gambit.description: Area attack. All enemies in zone take damage and gain [Disoriented].

exploit.name: I Find Your Lack of Faith Disturbing
exploit.description: Once per scene, automatically succeed on a social attack against any non-Force user. They comply or suffer.

threatBuild.notes: Campaign nemesis. NOT meant to be fought at this tier — he is an orbital threat. Power 8 = (Ref 5-1)+4. If the heroes face him directly, they will die. He is the reason to run.

scenes.hazards: Star Destroyer in orbit. Time pressure. Draco's willingness to destroy the entire city.

decisions.consequence: Must win the pot before Draco acts. High pressure.
Skips the tournament but alerts everyone. Extremely dangerous without the credits.

skillChecks.context: Finalizing tournament entry with fabricated credentials.
skillChecks.success: Entry secured. No flags raised.
skillChecks.failure: Minor discrepancy noted. Casino staff will verify — check may come back during the tournament.

skillChecks.context: Planning the overall heist approach based on available intel.
skillChecks.success: Clear plan with contingencies. Each team member knows their role.
skillChecks.failure: Plan has gaps. Improvisation will be needed.

skillChecks.context: Scanning the system for the Assiduous's capabilities.
skillChecks.success: Star Destroyer identified. Full sensor profile. Draco is aboard.
skillChecks.failure: Confirm it's an ISD. Details unclear.

skillChecks.context: Staying focused despite the escalation — Star Destroyer in orbit.
skillChecks.success: Steel nerves. The plan holds.
skillChecks.failure: [Rattled] from the weight of the stakes. -1 Power on first tournament check.

environmentMechanics.name: The Clock
environmentMechanics.effect: Draco's patience is finite. The GM tracks escalation: Day 1-2 = observation. Day 3 = suspicion. Day 4+ = action.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Win fast. Get out faster.

rewards.narrative: Tournament entry secured. The clock is running.

pacing.openingBeat: Final preparations. Then the Assiduous drops out of hyperspace.
pacing.risingAction: Varth confirms: Inquisitor Draco is aboard. The stakes are existential.
pacing.climax: The decision: go forward with the plan or abort.
pacing.resolution: The heroes commit. Tournament begins tomorrow.

scenes.title: The Elimination Rounds
scenes.subtitle: Days 1 & 2
scenes.readAloud:
The Royal Casino is a loud, smoky, aggressive establishment where prospectors gamble away fortunes and refugees bet their last credits. Over fifty competitors crowd the tables. The air is thick with Corellian whiskey, Ryll spice smoke, and the mechanical whir of shuffling droids.

You notice two players worth watching: a flamboyant human named Silas Draver who seems drunk but isn't, and a cold Hapan noblewoman named Lady Fioro who bankrupts a weeping Dug in the first hour.
scenes.gmNotes:
Days 1-2 of the tournament. The heroes identify key players:
- Lt. Arandis at Table 4: plays like a machine. Folds strong hands, raises weak ones against specific opponents.
- Silas Draver: loud and arrogant but stone cold sober. Casing the room and checking camera sightlines. He's planning a heist.
- Lady Mirana Fioro: icy Hapan noblewoman. Keeps glancing at Arandis with calculation — she's Darga's proxy.

Varth coaches via comms: 'Watch who Arandis loses to. He's not allowed to lose to anyone but Darga's proxy.'

npcs.name: Silas Draver
npcs.notes: Posing as a drunk gambler. Actually casing the room for a robbery.

passive.name: Always Working
passive.description: Draver is always casing, always planning. He knows every exit, every camera, every guard rotation.

actions.name: Quick Draw
actions.description: Pull and fire a concealed heavy blaster. Surprises on first round.

actions.name: Smug Deflection
actions.description: Social attack disguised as charm. Disarms suspicion.

maneuver.name: Case the Room
maneuver.description: Identify one tactical advantage in the current environment.

gambit.name: Signal
gambit.description: Activate planted operatives. Disguised thugs reveal themselves.

exploit.name: Dead Man's Bluff
exploit.description: When cornered, threaten to destroy the prize (Fioro/datapad). Forces negotiation.

threatBuild.notes: The third-party wildcard. Agile: +1 Evasion. He's been planning this heist for months. The heroes don't see him coming.

npcs.name: Lady Mirana Fioro
npcs.notes: Icy and ruthless. The person Arandis is supposed to lose to.

passive.name: Ice Queen
passive.description: Immune to [Rattled] and [Shaken]. Fioro doesn't crack under pressure.

maneuver.name: Compose
maneuver.description: Remove one social condition from self.

gambit.name: Reveal Identity
gambit.description: Invoke Hapan nobility. Guards defer. Civilians back away.

exploit.name: Insurance
exploit.description: If captured, reveal Darga's location in exchange for freedom.

threatBuild.notes: Darga's proxy. Not a fighter. Her value is the credits she holds and the information she'll spill under pressure.

npcs.name: Various Competitors
npcs.notes: Species with telepathy, cybernetic implants, alien poker faces.

passive.name: Background Noise
passive.description: Fifty competitors provide social cover. Heroes blend in.

actions.name: Gamble
actions.description: Play Sabacc. Some are good, most aren't.

maneuver.name: Gossip
maneuver.description: Share or spread rumors about other players.

threatBuild.notes: Color NPCs. Not individually important. Provide atmosphere and social cover.

scenes.hazards: Surveillance systems. Smugglers with hidden weapons. Spice deals. Imperial undercover agents.

decisions.consequence: Advance in the tournament but may miss crucial intelligence.

decisions.choice: Focus on gathering intel on Arandis and Fioro
decisions.consequence: Better prepared for the heist but risk elimination from the tournament.

skillChecks.context: Playing Sabacc — maintaining a poker face while reading opponents.
skillChecks.success: Win the hand. Advance in the tournament.
skillChecks.failure: Lose the hand. Credits lost. Risk of elimination.

skillChecks.context: Reading other players — identifying Darga's proxy.
skillChecks.success: Lady Fioro keeps glancing at Arandis. She's the proxy.
skillChecks.failure: Too many players to read. Can't identify the connection.

skillChecks.context: Analyzing Arandis's playing pattern between hands.
skillChecks.success: He folds strong hands against everyone except Fioro. The pattern is mathematical.
skillChecks.failure: His play seems random. No clear pattern.

skillChecks.context: Slipping away from the table to investigate Draver's activities.
skillChecks.success: Draver is checking camera sightlines and testing dealer droid response times. He's planning a heist.
skillChecks.failure: Draver notices your interest. Plays drunker to throw you off.

skillChecks.context: Building rapport with other players to gather intel.
skillChecks.success: A talkative Bothan reveals: Fioro arrived on a ship registered to Darga's company.
skillChecks.failure: Other players are guarded. Tournament atmosphere is tense.

environmentMechanics.name: Tournament Structure
environmentMechanics.effect: Each day, half the players are eliminated. Heroes must win enough hands to survive while gathering intel.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Varth coaches via encrypted comms. +1 tier on one Sabacc check per day.

environmentMechanics.name: Casino Surveillance
environmentMechanics.effect: Cameras everywhere. Suspicious behavior is flagged by security.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Act natural. Casino security is looking for cheaters, not spies.

pacing.openingBeat: The Royal Casino. Fifty players. Smoke and tension.
pacing.risingAction: Days 1-2 of play. Players identified. Patterns emerge.
pacing.climax: Draver's suspicious behavior. Fioro's connection to Arandis.
pacing.resolution: Advancement to the next round. Intel gathered.

scenes.title: The Pressure Cooker
scenes.subtitle: Day 3
scenes.readAloud:
Day three. Only two tables remain. The buy-ins have escalated, and the audience has shifted from celebration to funeral silence.

Midway through the session, a Devaronian gambler named Szygar goes bust against Arandis. He snaps, leaping up and screaming accusations of cheating. A hold-out blaster appears in his hand.

Panic ripples through the crowd. Arandis doesn't flinch.
scenes.gmNotes: The Szygar incident is a test for the heroes. If working security, they must subdue him without causing a casino shutdown. If playing, they must decide whether to intervene. If heroes don't act, Arandis's undercover Stormtroopers will vaporize Szygar, potentially causing a police investigation that cancels the tournament. Varth: 'Don't let them shoot! scenes.title: The Final Table
scenes.subtitle: Day 4
scenes.readAloud:
The final day. Five players remain under a harsh spotlight: Arandis, Fioro, Draver, the Rodian Creeska, and you.

Arandis plays strangely — folding strong hands against everyone except Fioro. He goes All In against her. She calls.

He reveals a losing hand. The crowd erupts. But instead of sweeping his cards away, Arandis deliberately arranges them on the table: One of Coins. Three of Sabers. Seven of Flasks.

Varth decodes it instantly: "1-3-7. That's a frequency — 137.12. It's the slave circuit control code for the Shackles of Nizon. He just handed her the keys to the getaway car AND the money."
scenes.gmNotes: The climax of the game. Arandis deliberately loses the massive pot to Fioro (Darga's proxy), transferring the Imperial payment. The card arrangement is a code — the slave circuit frequency for the Shackles of Nizon. Fioro now has the credits (digitally transferred to a datapad) and the ship code. The heroes must grab her before she leaves. This is the moment the heist transitions to action.

scenes.title: The Heist
scenes.subtitle: Combat
scenes.readAloud:
Just as you move to intercept Fioro, Silas Draver makes his play. He stands, pulls a heavy blaster, and fires into the ceiling.

"Nobody moves! The Empire isn't the only one getting paid today!"

The dealer droids spin around, revealing hidden blaster cannons. Draver's men — disguised as waiters and guests — pull weapons and secure the exits. Draver grabs Fioro, pressing a blaster to her temple.

"The datapad. Now."
scenes.gmNotes: Three-way crossfire in a crowded casino. Draver weaponized the dealer droids and planted his men as staff. The heroes are caught between Draver's heist crew, panicking civilians, and Arandis who draws a concealed military blaster to protect the transaction's secrecy. Arandis likely dies in the crossfire, removing the Imperial witness. Varth: 'Forget the civilians! Get the datapad!' After defeating Draver, Fioro spills Darga's location: Docking Bay 4414.

scenes.title: The Aftermath
scenes.readAloud:
The casino is a wreck. Sirens wail in the distance. Lady Fioro, wounded and terrified, spills everything.

"He's at the lower docks — Docking Bay 4414. He has the Shackles of Nizon holding in orbit on slave circuits. He's taking the gas and the slaves and leaving the sector tonight!"

Varth scans the system. His voice is grim: "The Shackles just activated its engines. And... damn it. The Assiduous just entered the system. Draco is here. Get to the hangar. If Darga launches, we lose everything."
scenes.gmNotes: Fioro reveals Darga's location and the Shackles of Nizon's status. Varth confirms Draco has entered the system. The heroes must flee the crime scene and race through Cloud City to the industrial docks before Darga escapes. This is a transition scene — setting up Part 3.

scenes.subtitle: Streets of Cloud City
scenes.readAloud:
The journey to the lower docks is a sprint through a city in lockdown. Darga has bribed the Wing Guard to declare you 'Enemy Saboteurs.'

The chase transitions from pristine white corridors into the steam-filled, rusted catwalks of the Industrial District. Tibanna gas vents hiss, obscuring vision, and the orange glow of the Bespin sunset filters through heavy industrial grates.

Varth hacks door controls from the ship: "Left at the junction! No, the other left! I'm jamming their comms, but I can't stop them from shooting at you. Move!"
scenes.gmNotes: Running battle through Cloud City. The heroes are pinned down by corrupt Wing Guard and Stormtroopers. This is a chase — stopping to fight every enemy means being overwhelmed. Use the environment: shoot steam pipes to scald pursuers, jump onto moving cargo skiffs, cut through maintenance ducts. Varth acts as 'The Man in the Chair,' hacking door controls.

scenes.title: Hutt's Hangar
scenes.subtitle: Docking Bay 4414
scenes.readAloud:
You kick open the blast doors to Docking Bay 4414. The cavernous hangar is open to the roiling clouds of Bespin on one side, wind howling through the magnetic field.

Dominating the center is Darga's personal luxury yacht — the Glorious Chariot. Gold and purple, engines whining with a turbine squeal that vibrates in your teeth. Six Z-95 Headhunters idle on the deck, pilots scrambling into cockpits.

Darga stands at the base of the ramp, screaming at his loading crews. When he sees you, fear becomes rage.

"Kill them! They brought the Inquisitor down on us! I want their heads!"
scenes.gmNotes: The final confrontation with Darga. The Glorious Chariot is priming for launch. Six Z-95 fighters are scrambling — if they get airborne, they'll strafe the hangar. Savvy heroes might shoot pilots on ladders to hijack fighters. Darga retreats up the ramp as mercenaries fall. Heroes must breach the ship or disable its engines. If they board, they fight Darga in the yacht's narrow corridors where his massive bulk is a weapon.

environmentMechanics.effect: Open to Bespin's atmosphere. Characters pushed past the boundary fall.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay away from the edge. Or push enemies over it.

environmentMechanics.name: Fuel Crates
environmentMechanics.effect: [Hazard 3] explosion in zone. Major area denial.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Tactical positioning. Use explosions offensively.

rewards.narrative: Hangar contested. Z-95s prevented from launching (or hijacked). Darga on the yacht.

pacing.openingBeat: Blast doors open. The Glorious Chariot dominates the bay.
pacing.risingAction: Z-95s scrambling. Security droid advancing. Thugs opening fire.
pacing.climax: Reach the yacht before Darga seals the ramp.
pacing.resolution: Hangar control established (or not). The firefight continues on the ship.

scenes.title: The Firefight
scenes.readAloud:
The hangar erupts into chaos. Blaster bolts ricochet off hull plating and fuel crates. The Z-95 engines roar to life one by one. Darga, seeing his mercenaries fall, turns and lumbers up the boarding ramp of the Glorious Chariot.

The yacht's engines begin their warm-up cycle. If he seals that ramp, he's gone.
scenes.gmNotes: The climactic ground fight. Heroes must cross the hangar using fuel crates and machinery for cover. Primary objective: stop the Glorious Chariot from launching. If Z-95s get airborne, they strafe the hangar floor. If heroes breach the yacht, they fight Darga in opulent but narrow corridors. Darga fights desperately — he knows this is his last stand.

npcs.name: Darga the Hutt
npcs.notes: Retreating up the ramp. Will fight in the ship's corridors.

passive.name: Absolute Authority
passive.description: All allies in Darga's court gain +1 to Resist social attacks. His presence is law.

actions.name: Command
actions.description: Order one ally to immediately attack or take an action.

actions.name: Crushing Tail
actions.description: Melee attack at engaged range. +2 Power. Hutt mass.

maneuver.name: Hover-Sled
maneuver.description: Move on repulsor sled. Not impeded by terrain.

gambit.name: Example
gambit.description: Order guards to execute a prisoner or destroy property. All witnesses gain [Shaken].

exploit.name: Bounty
exploit.description: Declare a bounty on one target. All mercenaries in the fortress gain +1 Power vs that target for the scene.

threatBuild.notes: Darga is a crime lord, not a fighter. Boss x2 Vitality = 24 = (5+4+3)*2. Power 7 via Commander (Presence 5-1)+3. He fights through his army. Direct combat is a mistake — he's a Hutt on a hover-sled surrounded by guards. (Recurring — same stat block from Adv2. Now desperate and enraged.) (Final fight. Desperate and dangerous.)

npcs.name: Remaining Mercenaries
npcs.notes: Fighting to cover Darga's retreat.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

maneuver.name: Take Cover
maneuver.description: Move to cover.

exploit.name: Last Stand
exploit.description: When Darga falls, mercs either surrender or fight to the death (50/50).

threatBuild.notes: Generic minion mercenaries. Darga's last loyal muscle.

scenes.hazards: Fuel crate explosions. Z-95 engine wash. Narrow yacht corridors if boarding.

decisions.consequence: Close-quarters fight with Darga in his own ship. Can capture the yacht.
Safer but Darga might have an escape pod.

skillChecks.context: Close-quarters combat with Darga in the yacht's corridors.
skillChecks.success: Decisive blow. Darga takes heavy damage.
skillChecks.failure: Darga's bulk fills the corridor. He crushes you against the wall.

skillChecks.context: Surviving Darga's tail whip in tight quarters.
skillChecks.success: Absorb the blow. Stay standing.
skillChecks.failure: Knocked into bulkhead. [Stunned] for 1 round.

skillChecks.context: Disabling the Glorious Chariot's engines from inside.
skillChecks.success: Engines shut down. The yacht isn't going anywhere.
skillChecks.failure: Engines spool up. Must be disabled from the cockpit.

skillChecks.context: Clearing mercenaries from the hangar floor.
skillChecks.success: Mercs go down. Clear path to the yacht.
skillChecks.failure: Mercs hold their ground. Must be flanked.

encounters.name: Darga's Last Stand
encounters.trigger: Heroes board the yacht or Darga's mercs are defeated
encounters.description: Darga fights desperately in the yacht's corridors. His massive bulk is a weapon in the tight space. Remaining mercs cover his retreat.
encounters.tactics: Darga uses Crushing Tail in corridors where heroes can't dodge. Commands remaining forces. He knows this is the end.

environmentMechanics.name: Narrow Yacht Corridors
environmentMechanics.effect: Only 1 character can engage Darga at a time. His bulk fills the corridor. No flanking.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Ranged attacks from behind the front fighter. Or disable from the engine room.

environmentMechanics.name: Fuel Crate Chain Reaction
environmentMechanics.effect: Sequential explosions across the bay. Characters must Athletics to dodge.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't start the chain. Or use it to clear the bay.

environmentMechanics.name: Z-95 Engine Wash
environmentMechanics.effect: Jet wash creates [Hazard 2] zones behind active fighters. Hearing is impossible in affected zones.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay clear of launch lanes.

items.name: Darga's Personal Datapad
items.note: Project Leviathan files, shipping records, Imperial contacts.

rewards.narrative: Darga defeated. The Glorious Chariot is captured. Act I climax approaching.

pacing.openingBeat: The hangar erupts. Blaster fire and engine roar.
pacing.risingAction: Darga retreats to the yacht. Mercs cover him.
pacing.climax: Boarding the yacht. Close-quarters fight with a Hutt.
pacing.resolution: Darga falls. The yacht is secured.

scenes.title: Grand Theft Starship
scenes.readAloud:
With Darga defeated and the hangar secured, you barely have time to breathe before Varth barks over the comms:

"Imperial stormtroopers have breached the elevator shaft! You have thirty seconds before a platoon comes through that door. You can't go back the way you came. Take the ships! Steal the yacht! I'll rendezvous with you in orbit!"
scenes.gmNotes: The heroes commandeer the Glorious Chariot and any intact Z-95 Headhunters. As hangar doors blast open and Stormtroopers pour in, the heroes punch the throttles and blast out of the hangar bay into the orange clouds of Bespin. This transitions to the orbital battle.

npcs.name: Stormtrooper Platoon
npcs.notes: Breaching the elevator shaft. 30 seconds until arrival.

passive.name: Disciplined Fire
passive.description: +1 Power on weapon attacks.

maneuver.name: Advance
maneuver.description: Breach and clear.

gambit.name: Suppressive Fire
gambit.description: Target gains [Pinned].

exploit.name: Overwhelming Numbers
exploit.description: When 20 troopers are present, all gain +2 Power.

threatBuild.notes: A full platoon. NOT meant to be fought. This is the "why you must launch NOW" threat. 20 minions with Overwhelming Numbers = TPK.

scenes.hazards: 30-second countdown. Stormtrooper platoon incoming. Must launch immediately.

decisions.consequence: Luxury yacht with decent shields and weapons. The primary escape vehicle.
Additional firepower for the orbital battle. Requires pilots.

skillChecks.context: Hot-starting the Glorious Chariot under fire.
skillChecks.success: Engines roar. The yacht lifts off immediately.
skillChecks.failure: Engine sputter. Delayed 1 round. Stormtroopers breach the hangar.

skillChecks.context: Activating unfamiliar Z-95 fighter systems.
skillChecks.success: Weapons hot. Systems green. Ready to fly.
skillChecks.failure: Unknown control layout. -1 Power on first piloting check.

skillChecks.context: Deciding which ships to take — yacht, fighters, or both.
skillChecks.success: Optimal loadout identified. Maximum firepower for the escape.
skillChecks.failure: Rushed decision. May leave useful assets behind.

encounters.name: 30-Second Countdown
encounters.description: Heroes have 30 seconds (5 rounds) to launch before 20 Stormtroopers breach. Hot-start the yacht and fighters, then blast out.
encounters.tactics: No time to fight. Every action must go toward launching. If not airborne by round 5, overwhelming force arrives.

environmentMechanics.name: 30-Second Clock
environmentMechanics.effect: Each round is 6 seconds. Round 5 = Stormtrooper platoon breaches. Round 6+ = overwhelmed.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Focus entirely on launch preparations. No side tasks.

items.name: The Glorious Chariot
items.note: Darga's luxury yacht. Now the heroes' ship.

items.name: Z-95 Headhunters
items.note: Up to 4 hijacked fighters (depending on how many were stopped from launching).

rewards.narrative: Airborne. Breaking atmosphere. The orbital battle begins.

pacing.openingBeat: Varth screams: "Stormtroopers! 30 seconds! TAKE THE SHIPS!"
pacing.climax: Blast doors opening. White armor pouring in.
pacing.resolution: Engines fire. The yacht punches through the magnetic field into orange clouds.

scenes.title: Skirmish Over Bespin
scenes.readAloud:
You break atmosphere and the sensor board lights up. The Imperial Star Destroyer Assiduous hangs in high orbit, launching TIE Fighters. The Shackles of Nizon — a transport filled with slaves — drifts dead in space. A massive automated Tibanna Gas Hauler is burning hard on a collision course with the slave ship.

Varth's voice is cold and professional:

"Good work getting airborne. That Gas Hauler is going to impact the transport in less than two minutes. It's a tragedy, but it's not our tragedy. We have the credits, we have the data. Spool up the hyperdrives. Let's get out of here while we're rich."

Then a frantic transmission cuts through:

"Varth! I'm on the transport! The slave circuits are locked! I can't steer and sensors show collision imminent! Where is that backup code?!"

It's Captain Okeefe.
scenes.gmNotes: The moral climax of Act I. Varth sent Okeefe to the slave ship intending for her to be destroyed with the evidence — she's a loose end. The Empire is using the Gas Hauler as a ram to destroy the Shackles of Nizon. Varth's position: 'She knew the risks. We jump. Now.' The heroes must choose: follow Varth and leave Okeefe to die, or turn and fight. If they fight, they engage TIE Fighters to destroy the Gas Hauler. Varth eventually helps — not from altruism, but because he needs the heroes alive for the fortune.

npcs.name: Captain Okeefe
npcs.notes: Trapped on the Shackles of Nizon. Varth intended for her to die.

passive.name: Veteran Smuggler
passive.description: Piloting checks in her ship gain +1 Power. Immune to [Rattled] from Imperial threats.

maneuver.name: Evasive
maneuver.description: Dodge incoming fire. Ship gains [Cover 1] for 1 round.

exploit.name: Last-Second Save
exploit.description: When the code is transmitted, Okeefe can steer the Shackles clear of the Hauler.

threatBuild.notes: Recurring ally. Same base stats as Adv1. Trapped on the Shackles of Nizon — can't fight, can only fly if the slave circuits are freed.

npcs.name: TIE Fighter Wing
npcs.notes: Screening the Gas Hauler. Must be engaged to reach it.

passive.name: Swarm Tactics
passive.description: For every 2 TIEs engaging the same target, all gain +1 Power.

maneuver.name: Intercept
maneuver.description: Move to engage a specific target.

gambit.name: Attack Run
gambit.description: Dive attack. +2 Power but [Vulnerable] until next turn.

exploit.name: Collision Course
exploit.description: When destroyed, debris may damage nearby ships.

threatBuild.notes: Ship-scale minions. Glass cannons — high evasion, low vitality. Masterful+ = destroyed. Dangerous in numbers via Swarm Tactics.

scenes.hazards: Star Destroyer in orbit. TIE Fighter screen. Automated Gas Hauler on collision course. Two-minute countdown.

decisions.choice: Turn and fight to save Okeefe
decisions.consequence: Engage TIEs and destroy the Gas Hauler. Varth reluctantly helps. Heroic but dangerous.

decisions.choice: Follow Varth's order and jump to hyperspace
decisions.consequence: Okeefe dies. The slaves die. The heroes are rich but compromised.

narrativeLinks.note: Okeefe was introduced as a reliable ally. Varth's willingness to sacrifice her reveals his true nature.

skillChecks.context: Dogfighting TIE Fighters while racing to destroy the Gas Hauler.
skillChecks.success: Outmaneuver TIEs. Clear firing line on the Hauler.
skillChecks.failure: TIEs on your tail. Taking fire. Must shake them first.

skillChecks.context: Destroying the automated Gas Hauler before it rams the slave ship.
skillChecks.success: Direct hit. The Hauler breaks apart. Okeefe is safe.
skillChecks.failure: Glancing hit. Hauler is damaged but still on collision course. Need another shot.

skillChecks.context: Transmitting the slave circuit code to free the Shackles of Nizon.
skillChecks.success: Circuits unlocked. Okeefe regains helm control.
skillChecks.failure: Transmission interference from the Star Destroyer. Must get closer.

skillChecks.context: Resisting Varth's pressure to jump and leave Okeefe.
skillChecks.success: You defy Varth. Your ship, your choice.
skillChecks.failure: Varth's logic is compelling. Hesitation costs a round.

encounters.name: Orbital Battle Over Bespin
encounters.description: TIE Fighter screen between the heroes and the Gas Hauler. 2-minute countdown to collision with the slave ship. Star Destroyer in the background.
encounters.tactics: TIEs screen the Hauler. Must punch through or go around. The Hauler is automated — no pilot to negotiate with. Must be destroyed before impact.

environmentMechanics.name: Collision Timer
environmentMechanics.effect: 2-minute countdown (20 rounds). When it hits 0, the Hauler rams the Shackles of Nizon. Everyone aboard dies.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Destroy the Hauler. Or transmit the slave circuit code so Okeefe can steer clear.

environmentMechanics.name: Star Destroyer Presence
environmentMechanics.effect: The Assiduous fires turbolaser batteries at long range. Random [Hazard 2] in one zone per round.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Don't stay in one zone. Keep moving.

environmentMechanics.name: Bespin's Atmosphere
environmentMechanics.effect: Tibanna gas clouds provide cover but also turbulence. Piloting check or [Disoriented].
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stay above the cloud layer unless desperate.

rewards.narrative: Gas Hauler destroyed. Okeefe saved (or abandoned). The moral choice that defines Act I.

pacing.openingBeat: Break atmosphere. Sensors light up. TIEs, Star Destroyer, and a collision countdown.
pacing.risingAction: TIE Fighter dogfight. Racing to reach the Gas Hauler.
pacing.climax: The shot — destroy the Hauler before it rams the slave ship.
pacing.resolution: Explosion. Okeefe's voice: "Slave circuits fried. We're jumping."

scenes.title: The Choice
scenes.subtitle: End of Act I
scenes.readAloud:
The Tibanna Gas Hauler explodes in a massive fireball just meters from the slave ship. Okeefe's voice, shaking with relief: "Slave circuits fried. Nav-computer is green. We are jumping to the rendezvous..."

A pause.

"...and Varth? You and I are going to have a very long talk."

The Assiduous opens fire with turbolasers. Green bolts flash past your cockpit. Varth screams:

"We overstayed our welcome! PUNCH IT!"

The stars stretch into lines and you're gone.
scenes.gmNotes: The aftermath of Act I. At the rendezvous: Okeefe punches Varth in the jaw. He takes it, claiming it was a 'tactical calculation that changed.' Rewards: The Glorious Chariot, Z-95 fighters, tournament winnings, Darga's assets, Project Leviathan files. Varth's tease: 'This data points to a massive construction project in the Unknown Regions. If you want to know what the Empire is really building, we have to go off the map.' This concludes Act I and sets up the campaign's second act.

npcs.name: Admiral Varth
npcs.notes: Takes a punch from Okeefe. Plays it off. Already planning the next move.

passive.name: The Man in the Chair
passive.description: Varth provides remote tactical support. +1 tier on one skill check per scene when he can communicate.

actions.name: Tactical Analysis
actions.description: Provide intel on an enemy or situation.

maneuver.name: Issue Orders
maneuver.description: One ally gains a bonus maneuver.

gambit.name: Calculated Sacrifice
gambit.description: Designate one ally as "expendable" for the scene. That ally gains +2 Power but Varth won't help them if they go down.

exploit.name: Imperial Codes
exploit.description: Once per adventure, use Imperial command codes to cause a brief disruption — communications blackout, door overrides, etc.

threatBuild.notes: Varth is the party's handler, not a fighter. His "Calculated Sacrifice" gambit is mechanically reflective of his character — he will sacrifice allies for the mission. The heroes should notice this pattern.

npcs.name: Captain Okeefe
npcs.notes: Furious. Punches Varth. Trust is deeply damaged.

passive.name: Veteran Smuggler
passive.description: Piloting checks in her ship gain +1 Power. Immune to [Rattled] from Imperial threats.

maneuver.name: Evasive
maneuver.description: Dodge incoming fire. Ship gains [Cover 1] for 1 round.

exploit.name: Last-Second Save
exploit.description: When the code is transmitted, Okeefe can steer the Shackles clear of the Hauler.

threatBuild.notes: Recurring ally. Same base stats as Adv1. Trapped on the Shackles of Nizon — can't fight, can only fly if the slave circuits are freed. (Recurring. Furious at Varth. Trust deeply damaged.)

scenes.hazards: Turbolaser fire from the Assiduous. Must jump to hyperspace immediately.

decisions.consequence: He deflects — 'tactical calculation.' But the heroes now know what he's capable of.

skillChecks.context: Jumping to hyperspace under turbolaser fire.
skillChecks.success: Clean jump. Stars stretch into lines.
skillChecks.failure: Glancing turbolaser hit during jump. Ship takes damage but enters hyperspace.

skillChecks.context: Reading Varth after the Okeefe betrayal. What is he really?
skillChecks.success: Cold. Calculating. He didn't hesitate to sacrifice her. He will do it again if the math works.
skillChecks.failure: His explanation seems reasonable — "tactical calculation." But something doesn't sit right.

skillChecks.context: Confronting Varth about sending Okeefe to die.
skillChecks.success: Varth drops the mask for a moment. "I did what I thought was necessary. I was wrong." Genuine or calculated? Hard to tell.
skillChecks.failure: Varth deflects cleanly. "Tactical calculation that changed. You made a different call. I respect that." Smooth as durasteel.

skillChecks.context: Processing the emotional weight of Act I's events.
skillChecks.success: Steel yourself. Ready for whatever comes next.
skillChecks.failure: [Rattled] carries into the next adventure. The weight lingers.

environmentMechanics.name: Turbolaser Fire
environmentMechanics.effect: Assiduous opens fire. Random [Hazard 3] per round until jump.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Jump immediately. Don't linger.

items.name: The Glorious Chariot
items.note: Darga's luxury yacht. The party's new ship.

items.name: Project Leviathan Files
items.note: Data pointing to a massive construction project in the Unknown Regions.

rewards.narrative: Act I concludes. The heroes have Darga's fortune, his ship, and critical intelligence. But they also know what Varth is capable of. The campaign shifts from "survive and profit" to "what is the Empire building in the Unknown Regions?"

pacing.openingBeat: The Gas Hauler explodes. Okeefe is safe. The Assiduous opens fire.
pacing.climax: At the rendezvous: Okeefe punches Varth.
pacing.resolution: Act I ends. Varth teases Act II: "If you want to know what the Empire is really building, we have to go off the map."
